description_long: This is a two-story apartment building with a hipped roof covered in dark shingles and a facade of light gray brick. The structure features large, symmetrically arranged rectangular windows on both floors, providing ample natural light to the interior. The front side has a central single door flanked by two large windows, while the rear includes a protruding section with its own entrance and additional windows. The building's roof extends slightly beyond the walls, creating subtle eaves all around. There are no visible balconies, and the overall design is simple and functional, typical of contemporary suburban or urban residential architecture. The exterior materials suggest durability and low maintenance, with a focus on practicality and comfort.
description: A two-story, light gray brick apartment building with a dark hipped roof, large rectangular windows, and a simple, symmetrical facade.
description_view_0: The front view shows a central single door, two large windows on either side, and three upper windows, all set in a light gray brick wall beneath a dark hipped roof.
description_view_1: This side view reveals a long wall with several evenly spaced windows on both floors and no visible entrances or protrusions.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a protruding section with its own entrance and windows, as well as additional windows on the main building, all under the hipped roof.
description_view_3: The opposite side view shows a similar arrangement to view 1, with a small side extension and multiple windows but no main entrance.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are symmetrically arranged in horizontal rows on both floors, with consistent spacing and alignment across all sides of the building.
